what_it_does: The host HTML page for the 'Vi' AI-workspace UI kit. Loads the shared color/type stylesheet plus the kit's vi.css, mounts a #app root, pulls React 18, ReactDOM, and Babel-standalone from unpkg CDN, and loads six text/babel screen scripts (ViMark, ViStatusPill, ChatPanel, TaskTree, OutputPreview, ViKitApp) in dependency order to render the workspace in-browser.
observations: Carries a @dsCard HTML comment (group='UI Kit — Vi', name, subtitle='Vi workspace — chat + task tree + live brochure preview', viewport='1480x800') — a metadata annotation the design-system gallery reads.
Links two stylesheets: ../../colors_and_type.css (shared tokens) and vi.css (kit-local).
Loads React, ReactDOM, and Babel from unpkg CDN with SRI integrity hashes and crossorigin — pinned versions (react 18.3.1, babel 7.29.0).
Six type='text/babel' script tags loaded in a deliberate order, ending with ViKitApp.jsx (the composer/mount).
Uses in-browser Babel transpilation (text/babel) rather than a build step.
standouts: The @dsCard comment binds this file into the design-system gallery with a descriptive subtitle and target viewport.
subtitle reveals the kit's intent: 'chat + task tree + live brochure preview' — the three-pane Vi workspace concept.
conventions: Follows the kit pattern of CDN React + in-browser Babel + ordered global-script loading (matching the window.X registration seen in the screen .jsx files).
Uses SRI integrity hashes and crossorigin on all CDN scripts (good supply-chain hygiene).
Links the shared colors_and_type.css so the kit inherits the central token base.
concerns: Depends on external CDN (unpkg) and the development builds of React/ReactDOM — won't render offline and ships dev bundles.
In-browser Babel transpilation is fine for mockups but not a production load path.
Script order is load-order-fragile (ViKitApp must come last); no module system enforces it.
notes: The Vi AI-workspace kit's index page — CDN React + Babel, shared tokens + vi.css, and ordered babel scripts assembling a chat / task-tree / brochure-preview workspace, gallery-tagged via @dsCard.
questions: What does ViKitApp.jsx compose the six scripts into (the workspace layout)?
Is the CDN/dev-build setup intentional for mockups only, or expected to back a real workspace?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the @dsCard subtitle describes a chat+task-tree+brochure-preview workspace and the file loads exactly those parts to render it.
intention_signals: Stands up a self-contained, build-free page that renders the Vi AI-workspace design so it can be viewed and judged, while declaring itself to the design-system gallery via @dsCard.
novelty: It frames an AI-workspace concept (chat pane + task tree + live brochure preview) as a single gallery-tagged kit page.
contribution: Brings the Vi AI-workspace kit's host page — the assembly point that loads the chat/task-tree/preview screen scripts and registers the kit as a gallery card.
summary_for_embedding: The host HTML for the ConceptV 'Vi' AI-workspace UI kit, loading shared tokens, vi.css, CDN React/Babel, and six babel screen scripts (chat, task tree, preview) with a @dsCard gallery annotation.
intention_for_embedding: To render and gallery-register the Vi AI-workspace design (chat + task tree + live brochure preview) as a build-free browser mockup.
